title: Calonemorchis colorata
taxonomy: Calonemorchis colorata is a species of orchid. It is classified within the family Orchidaceae. Further classification places Calonemorchis colorata within the subfamily Orchidoideae. Within this subfamily, it belongs to the tribe Cranichideae. Its classification at the genus level is within the subtribe Orchidinae.
uses: Calonemorchis colorata is known to have some traditional medicinal uses, particularly in the Pacific Islands where it is native. It is used to treat various ailments, including fever, cough, and respiratory issues. The plant is also believed to have anti-inflammatory properties, which may help to soothe wounds and reduce pain. Additionally, it is sometimes used to treat digestive issues and to reduce swelling. However, it's essential to note that the effectiveness and safety of using Calonemorchis colorata for medicinal purposes have not been extensively scientifically studied.
properties: Calonemorchis colorata is a species of orchid native to Australia and New Guinea.
